title: Getting started with assert
text: assert gives small, explicit helpers checking inputs outputs functions. place assertions top function; one fails, throws error function stops real work happens. examples load package box::use(assert) call functions namespace, assert$assert_numeric(x), always clear function comes . library(assert) followed bare assert_numeric(x) works . Every assertion shares contract: checks one condition, returns input invisibly success, checks stack neatly, throws clear error pointing function failure.

title: Getting started with assert
text: Read top bottom, assertions describe preconditions: “values numeric, missing values; factor single number.” something wrong, message names argument calling function:
code: scale_values <- function(values, factor) {   assert$assert_numeric(values)   assert$assert_no_missing_values(values)   assert$assert_scalar_numeric(factor)    return(values * factor) }  scale_values(c(1, 2, 3), 10) #> [1] 10 20 30 scale_values(c(1, NA, 3), 10) #> Error in `scale_values()`: #> ! `values` must not contain missing values. scale_values(c(1, 2, 3), c(10, 20)) #> Error in `scale_values()`: #> ! `factor` must be a single numeric value.

title: Getting started with assert
text: assert_scalar_* check requires single, non-NA value, lone value secretly NA almost always mistake. plain vector checks (assert_numeric(), assert_character(), …) still allow NA, since missing value among many often legitimate.
code: assert$assert_scalar_numeric(NA) #> Error: #> ! `NA` must be a single numeric value.

title: Getting started with assert
text: Arguments default NULL optional. default assertion rejects NULL; pass null_ok = TRUE allow still validating value supplied.
code: greet <- function(name, title = NULL) {   assert$assert_scalar_character(name)   assert$assert_scalar_character(title, null_ok = TRUE)    return(if (is.null(title)) name else paste(title, name)) }  greet("Ada") #> [1] "Ada" greet("Lovelace", title = "Ms") #> [1] "Ms Lovelace"

title: Getting started with assert
text: Beyond types, can assert content vector.
code: assert$assert_all_positive(c(1, 2, 3)) assert$assert_all_within_range(c(0.2, 0.5, 0.9), minimum = 0, maximum = 1) assert$assert_values_in_set(c("buy", "sell"), c("buy", "sell", "hold")) invisible(NULL)

title: Getting started with assert
text: several optional arguments interact, argument-group helpers express rule directly. treat NULL “supplied”.
code: open_file <- function(path = NULL, connection = NULL) {   assert$assert_exactly_one(path, connection)   return("ok") }  open_file(path = "data.csv") #> [1] "ok" open_file()  # neither supplied #> Error in `open_file()`: #> ! Exactly one of these must be supplied: path, connection (0 were #>   supplied).

title: Getting started with assert
text: every assertion returns input invisibly, checks compose naturally native pipe |>. one validates value passes next, chain reads top bottom list guarantees object. works object, especially handy data frames, often want confirm structure column types one place.
code: trades <- data.frame(   symbol = c("AAA", "BBB"),   quantity = c(10L, 5L),   price = c(1.5, 2.0) )  trades |>   assert$assert_data_frame() |>   assert$assert_has_columns(c("symbol", "quantity", "price")) |>   assert$assert_column_types(list(symbol = "character", quantity = "integer", price = "numeric")) |>   assert$assert_unique_rows() |>   invisible()

title: Getting started with assert
text: condition without dedicated assertion, use assert_true() custom message.
code: set_threshold <- function(x) {   assert$assert_scalar_numeric(x)   assert$assert_true(x %% 2 == 0, message = "`x` must be an even number.")   return(x) }  set_threshold(4) #> [1] 4 set_threshold(3) #> Error in `set_threshold()`: #> ! `x` must be an even number.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Dereck Mezquita. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Mezquita, D. (2026). assert: Assert. R package version 0.0.0.9000. Available https://github.com/dereckscompany/assert
code: @Manual{assert-2026,   title = {assert: Assert},   author = {Dereck Mezquita},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.0.9000},   url = {https://github.com/dereckscompany/assert}, }

what: Readable Assertions for Validating Function Inputs and Outputs
title: Readable Assertions for Validating Function Inputs and Outputs
text: assert lightweight toolkit assertion helpers checking inputs outputs functions. Drop assertions top function get clear, immediate error argument wrong type, shape, value.

title: Readable Assertions for Validating Function Inputs and Outputs
text: Every assertion follows simple contract: Checks one thing. function tests single, explicit condition. Returns input invisibly. Checks can stacked, one per line, reading like checklist preconditions. Fails loudly clearly. failure throws error names offending argument points function, assert’s internals. Explicit, readable names. assert_scalar_character(), assert_chr().

title: Readable Assertions for Validating Function Inputs and Outputs
text: assert CRAN; install GitHub renv:
code: # first install renv itself if you don't have it: install.packages("renv") renv::install("dereckscompany/assert")

title: Readable Assertions for Validating Function Inputs and Outputs
text: Write assertions top function. one fails, throws error function stops immediately. input invalid, error names argument calling function:
code: box::use(assert)  average_price <- function(prices, weights) {   assert$assert_numeric(prices)   assert$assert_no_missing_values(prices)   assert$assert_all_positive(prices)   assert$assert_same_length(prices, weights)    return(sum(prices * weights) / sum(weights)) }  average_price(c(10, 20, 30), c(1, 1, 2)) #> [1] 22.5 average_price(c(10, -5, 30), c(1, 1, 2)) #> Error in `average_price()`: #> ! `prices` must contain only positive values (greater than zero).

title: Readable Assertions for Validating Function Inputs and Outputs
text: Arguments default NULL optional. Pass null_ok = TRUE let assertion accept NULL still checking value supplied:
code: connect <- function(host, port = NULL) {   assert$assert_scalar_character(host)   assert$assert_scalar_integer(port, null_ok = TRUE)   return(invisible(TRUE)) }  connect("localhost")          # port omitted — fine connect("localhost", 8080L)   # port supplied and valid — fine

title: Readable Assertions for Validating Function Inputs and Outputs
text: every assertion returns input invisibly, checks compose naturally native pipe |>. one validates value hands next, chain reads like list guarantees object:
code: people <- data.frame(name = c("Ada", "Alan"), age = c(36L, 41L))  people |>   assert$assert_data_frame() |>   assert$assert_has_columns(c("name", "age")) |>   assert$assert_column_types(list(name = "character", age = "integer")) |>   invisible()

title: Readable Assertions for Validating Function Inputs and Outputs

Defaults FALSE, NULL rejected unless opt . arg Name used refer x error messages. Defaults name expression passed x. call Environment used error's call context. Defaults calling function, errors point user's code.

code: assert_character(c("a", "b", "c")) assert_numeric(1:10) assert_list(list(1, "a", TRUE))

title: assert 0.0.1
text: First release assert, lightweight toolkit readable assertion helpers validating function inputs outputs. assertion checks single condition, returns input invisibly checks can stacked, throws clear, caller-aware error failure. Pass null_ok = TRUE allow optional arguments default NULL.
